routing.title: Routing
routing.description: Next.js uses a file-system based router built on top of React Server Components. Each folder in the app directory represents a route segment.
routing.basicsDesc: Link, Navigation, useRouter — the fundamentals of moving between pages.
routing.dynamicDesc: [slug] syntax — pages that respond to different URL parameters.
routing.catchAllDesc: [...slug] syntax — match any number of URL segments.
routing.groupsDesc: (folder) syntax — organize routes without affecting the URL.
routing.parallelDesc: @slot syntax — render multiple pages simultaneously in the same layout.
routing.interceptingDesc: (.) syntax — intercept routes to show modal overlays.

data.title: Data
data.description: Next.js provides multiple patterns for fetching, caching, and mutating data — from server-side async components to client-side queries.
data.fetchingDesc: Fetch data directly in async Server Components — no useEffect needed.
data.clientFetchDesc: Client-side data fetching with caching, refetching, and loading states.
data.cachingDesc: use cache directive — cache component and function results.
data.revalidationDesc: revalidatePath and revalidateTag — update cached data on demand.
data.serverActionsDesc: Form mutations with Server Actions — CRUD operations with Drizzle ORM.
data.streamingDesc: Suspense boundaries and loading UI — progressive page rendering.

rendering.title: Rendering
rendering.description: Next.js supports Server Components (default) and Client Components. Understanding when to use each is key to building performant apps.
rendering.serverComponentsDesc: React Server Components — render on the server, zero client JS.
rendering.clientComponentsDesc: 'use client' directive — interactive components with hooks and events.
rendering.compositionDesc: Server wrapping Client pattern — the best of both worlds.
rendering.stateDesc: Cross-component state management with Zustand in a Next.js app.

ui.title: UI & Assets
ui.description: Next.js provides built-in optimizations for images, fonts, and CSS. Combined with theming and animations, you can build beautiful, performant UIs.
ui.cssDesc: CSS Modules, Tailwind CSS, global styles — styling strategies in Next.js.
ui.imagesDesc: next/image component — automatic optimization, lazy loading, responsive images.
ui.fontsDesc: next/font — Google Fonts and local fonts with zero layout shift.
ui.metadataDesc: Static and dynamic metadata, Open Graph images, sitemap, robots.txt.
ui.animationsDesc: Motion library and View Transitions API for smooth page transitions.
ui.themesDesc: Dark/Light mode with next-themes — system preference and manual toggle.

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.description: Powerful features for production-grade applications — from middleware and authentication to internationalization and monitoring.
advanced.middlewareDesc: Run code before a request is completed — auth checks, redirects, headers.
advanced.i18nDesc: Multi-language support with next-intl — zh-TW and English.
advanced.authDesc: Authentication patterns — session management and route protection.
advanced.draftModeDesc: Preview unpublished content from your CMS before going live.
advanced.edgeRuntimeDesc: Run server code at the edge — lightweight and globally distributed.
advanced.errorHandlingDesc: Error boundaries with error.tsx — graceful error recovery.
advanced.mdxDesc: Write JSX in Markdown — interactive documentation and blog posts.
advanced.instrumentationDesc: OpenTelemetry integration — trace and monitor your application.
advanced.pwaDesc: Progressive Web App features — offline support and installability.
advanced.datesDesc: Date handling with dayjs — formatting, parsing, and i18n.

config.title: Config
config.description: next.config.ts is the control center for your Next.js app. Learn how to configure environment variables, redirects, headers, and analyze your bundle.
config.envDesc: NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix, .env files, runtime vs build-time variables.
config.redirectsDesc: URL redirects and rewrites in next.config.ts — routing without code.
config.headersDesc: Custom HTTP headers and Content Security Policy configuration.
config.bundleDesc: Visualize your JavaScript bundle — find and fix bloat.
